sections.heading: Education Facilities:
sections.para: BLW administration runs 1 BLW Inter College from class- 06 to 12 BLW Women's Welfare Organisation also runs a primary school Bal Niketan. BLW Township also possesses 1 Post Graduate Girl's College, 2 Central Schools, 1 St. John's school.

sections.heading: Medical Facilities:
sections.para: 105 bedded well-equipped Railway Hospital, a Health Unit and a First-Aid post in workshop provide comprehensive health care to around 20,000 Railway family members. A Homeopathic Clinic also available.

sections.heading: Railway Quarters:
sections.para: A total of 3704 staff quarters (including bunglow) are provided to the BLW employees. The Railway colony is well maintained and green, providing excellent pollution free environment.

sections.heading: Compassionate Appointments:
sections.para: Families of deceased employees are helped by the way of compassionate appointment to the wards.

sections.heading: Settlement Dues to retired employees and the families of the deceased employees:
sections.para: Special attention at highest level is given to settle the dues of the retired employees and the families of the deceased employees.

sections.heading: Institute/Community Centre etc.:
sections.para: For various social needs of the staff 1 Community Hall, 2 Institutes, 3 Barat Ghars, 1 Kalyan Kendra, 1 Rangshala, 1 Officers Club and 1 Staff Club are functioning on the premises.

sections.heading: Cinema Club:
sections.para: For recreation of staff a Cinema Hall of around 690 capacity is functioning since 1967 in BLW.

sections.heading: Sports & Game:
sections.para: All kinds of facilities for various games like Aquatics, Athletic, Two Indoor Badminton Court, Two Basketball Court with Flood Light, Health Club, Cricket Ground with Pavilion, Football Ground, Golf Course with 18 holes, Hockey Ground, Two Synthetic Lawn Tennis with Flood Light, Volleyball Court with Flood Light, Indoor Wrestling hall with two mat and one Akhara, Squash Hall and Handball Ground have been provided for the staff. Various Inter Railway Tournaments are regularly arranged in BLW premises.

complaintPolicy.title: Complaint Policy
complaintPolicy.description:
Complaint from public, various administrative authorities, NGOs, etc. are important sources of information for identification of vigilance related issues in the Railways. Complaints to the vigilance are thus meant to result in punitive action against the erring public servant(s). Relief as such in the matter to the complaint is only incidental to the vigilance action. Redressal of grievances vis-a-vis Government organizations or public sector enterprises should not be the focus of complaints to the vigilance.

Complaint can be lodged only against Railway officials over which Chief Vigilance Officer, Banaras Locomotive Works has jurisdiction. The vigilance department has no jurisdiction over private individuals and other Government department. Therefore, please do not lodge complaints against officials of these organizations to the Chief Vigilance Officer, Banaras Locomotive Works.

The complainant should note:
(i) The pre-requisite for investigation of a complaint, barring certain exceptions is that the complaint should be signed and contain the name and address of the complainant. Any complaint that does not bear the name and address of the complainant is an anonymous complaint. A complaint which does not bear the full particulars of the complainant or is unsigned or is not subsequently acknowledged by a complainant as having not been made is a pseudonymous complaint. The vigilance department will not entertain both anonymous and pseudonymous complaints.
(ii) The complainant for valid reasons desires that his identity be withheld while processing the complaint, the complaint may be addressed to Secretary, Central Vigilance Commission, New Delhi and should be superscribed 'Complaint Under the Public Interest Disclosure' on the envelope.

The Vigilance Department will not entertain or inquire into any disclosure –
(i) In respect of which a formal and public enquiry had been ordered under public servant's enquiries act, 1850 or
(ii) In respect of a matter which has been returned for enquiries under the commission of enquiries act, 1952.

Complaint should be addressed directly to the Chief Vigilance Officer, Banaras Locomotive Works. Complaints marked to a large number of functionaries are not normally pursued by the Chief Vigilance Officer.

Complaints must be specific and contain factual details, verifiable facts and related matters. It should not be vague or contain sweeping general allegations.

Only those complaints which are against officials within the jurisdiction of the Chief Vigilance Officer, Banaras Locomotive Works and have allegations of corruption will be investigated.

Other complaints will either be filed or will be referred to the department concerned for necessary administrative action.

Chief Vigilance Officer will not entertain unnecessary correspondence in the matter, but will ensure that the complaints are investigated and action taken to its logical conclusion.

In case the complaint is found to be motivated or vexatious, the Chief Vigilance Officer shall be at liberty to take appropriate steps.

Any corruption related complaint pertaining to Banaras Locomotive Works/Varanasi may be addressed to:
The Chief Vigilance Officer, Room No. 16, Vigilance Cell, Banaras Locomotive Works, Varanasi- 221004

BSNL Landline No.: 0542-2271853
Fax No.: 0542-2271853

For more information send your queries to cvo@dlw.railnet.gov.in

vigilanceOrganization.title: Vigilance Organization
vigilanceOrganization.intro: Vigilance department of Banaras Locomotive Works (BLW) is headed by the Chief Vigilance Officer (CVO), who is assisted by Deputy Chief Vigilance Officer (Dy.CVO)
vigilanceOrganization.appealText: If you have noticed any malpractice/corruption or have a suggestion to prevent/minimize malpractices pertaining to BLW, you may please inform the same to Vigilance Department, Banaras Locomotive Works, Varanasi, for necessary action.
vigilanceOrganization.address:
Chief Vigilance Officer(CVO),
Room No. 16, Vigilance Department,
Banaras Locomotive Works, Varanasi- 221004 (U.P.)
vigilanceOrganization.railMadad: Complaints can also be lodged over Phone on Rail Madad at 139
vigilanceOrganization.rtiNote: Voluntary Disclosure under Right to Information Act, 2005
